body:
## Implementation Plan Complete — BIN-72 AI Consent Controls

Plan written for privacy-first AI consent infrastructure.

### Summary

- **6 new files**: types/ai-consent.ts, lib/ai-consent.ts (consent state + activity log), AiPrivacySettings (settings UI), AiStoryPanel (per-story override)
- **2 modified files**: settings/page.tsx (new AI & Privacy section), story/[id]/page.tsx (sidebar + inline panel)
- Consent gate with checkbox + policy list; gating via `isAiEnabled()` that all future AI UI must check
- Toggle switch for global on/off; per-story select override (inherit/on/off)
- Activity log capped at 20 entries (empty state for now — no AI endpoints exist yet)
- Mobile: AiStoryPanel shown inline above editor canvas when sidebar hidden

See `<plan>` block in description for full types, CSS, and acceptance mapping.

Assign to: Code Monkey

## UX goal
Give writers clear, low-friction control over AI use so they feel safe enabling it. Consent should be explicit, understandable in under 10 seconds, and reversible at any time without hunting through the UI.

## Information hierarchy
1. Consent status at top: AI Off (not consented), AI On, or AI Restricted by story override.
2. Primary control: global AI default toggle in Settings.
3. Secondary control: per-story override in Story Editor context.
4. Explainers near action points: concise data-use notice (what is sent, why, retention).
5. Accountability: recent AI activity list (latest 20) with readable labels.

## Layout and components
- Settings page gets a new "AI & Privacy" group, visually matching existing `settingsGroup` blocks.
- Inside that group, stack three cards/rows:
  - Consent gate card: short policy summary + "I agree" checkbox + primary enable button.
  - Global default card: toggle switch with helper copy and immediate-save behavior.
  - Activity card: table/list with columns `Time`, `Story`, `Action`, `Status`; default sort newest first.
- Story editor page (`/story/[id]`) gets a compact per-story AI panel in the right sidebar (desktop) and inline collapsible panel above editor canvas (mobile/tablet where sidebar is hidden).
- AI-triggering UI (current/future) should include a small inline disclosure line directly below the control, with a "Learn more" link back to Settings > AI & Privacy.

## Interaction states
- Idle: show current consent and toggle states, with per-story badge: `Using global`, `Overridden on`, or `Overridden off`.
- Loading: disable toggles/buttons and show inline skeleton rows for activity list; preserve layout height to avoid jumps.
- Empty: activity list empty state copy: "No AI actions yet" + one-line explanation.
- Error: non-blocking inline alert above affected card, with retry action and preserved previous state.
- Success: subtle inline confirmation text (not modal/toast spam), e.g. "Saved" or "AI disabled for this story".

## Accessibility
- Keyboard: all toggles, checkbox, and "Learn more" controls reachable in logical tab order; ESC closes mobile disclosure/panel if modalized.
- Focus: keep existing 2px focus treatment style and ensure visible contrast on `--color-polar-100` and `--color-white` backgrounds.
- Contrast: body and helper text must stay at/above readable grayscale contrast; avoid using `--color-cement-400` for critical status messages.
- Text clarity: use plain-language labels ("Send story text to AI" instead of internal model terms).

## Visual style
- Stay within approved grayscale tokens in `src/styles/tokens.css`.
- Card surfaces: `--color-polar-100`; borders: `--color-fashion-300`; primary text: `--color-black-700`; secondary text: `--color-elephant-500`.
- Status chips should remain monochrome (light fill + darker text) to preserve the app’s calm writer-first tone.
- Keep typography aligned with existing settings and editor scales; no new color accents required for this ticket.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop (>=1024): AI per-story panel lives in editor sidebar; activity list uses multi-column row layout.
- Tablet/mobile (<1024 for editor sidebar behavior): per-story panel becomes inline/collapsible in editor flow; activity list collapses to stacked rows with label-value pairs.
- Settings controls should remain one-tap on mobile; avoid multi-step dialogs for consent after initial opt-in.

## Codebase alignment
Reviewed and aligned with current structure:
- `src/app/layout.tsx` and `src/components/AppShell/*` for shell, header, nav behavior.
- `src/app/settings/page.tsx` + `src/app/settings/page.module.css` for settings section/card pattern.
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` + `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` for editor/sidebar and mobile sidebar-hide breakpoint behavior.
- `src/styles/tokens.css` for grayscale token and spacing/typography constraints.

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse:
  - Existing settings group/card visual pattern and spacing rhythm.
  - Existing editor sidebar container and title/section styling language.
  - Existing focus style conventions used across inputs/buttons.
- New build:
  - AI consent card content block.
  - Global AI default toggle row (if no shared switch exists yet).
  - Per-story AI override control block.
  - AI activity list component with empty/loading/error states.

## Feasibility notes
- Global default + per-story override needs explicit precedence rule in UI copy: per-story setting wins when set; otherwise inherits global.
- Activity list usability depends on normalized action labels from backend (writer-readable action names, not internal event keys).
- First-use gating must be state-driven so AI actions are truly disabled before consent, not just visually hidden.

## Constraints / implementation notes
- Add AI/privacy surface primarily in `src/app/settings/page.tsx` and `src/app/settings/page.module.css` (new section reusing `settingsGroup` structure).
- Add per-story controls in `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` and `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css`, anchored to existing `.sidebarSection` on desktop and new inline block for `<1024px` where `.sidebar` is hidden.
- Any newly added button/input styles should consume tokens from `src/styles/tokens.css` only; avoid introducing non-token grays.
- Keep interaction copy concise and writer-facing to match `docs/INSPIRATION.md` clarity goals (Living Writer bar: clear, optional, unobtrusive AI).

## Handoff
Assign to: Founding Engineer

## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This ticket is worth approving because it moves us beyond generic "has AI" parity into trust-by-design: explicit consent gates, per-story controls, and visible AI activity history. Living Writer sets the bar for optional AI, but this scope can stand out by making consent and control more granular and auditable in day-to-day writing flow.

## Competitor evidence
- Living Writer positions AI as an optional capability and frames privacy/ownership as a core value in product messaging: https://livingwriter.com/ai-features/
- Living Writer Terms include dedicated AI Usage terms (including optional use and model/ownership constraints), showing this is already a competitive decision area: https://livingwriter.com/terms-conditions/
- Campfire’s FAQ references separate paths around Campfire/Blaze and a broad write/read/share ecosystem, but does not foreground per-story AI consent controls in the core writer flow: https://www.campfirewriting.com/faq

## Risks / trade-offs
- Added consent prompts can reduce first-use conversion if copy is verbose or interruptive.
- Per-story + global toggles can create state confusion without clear precedence rules.
- Activity logging adds UX and engineering complexity; value drops if logs are too technical for writers.

## Recommendation
Approve with design emphasis on clarity and low friction: default-off AI, one-tap explicit opt-in, clear global-vs-story precedence, and human-readable activity entries. This is a positive differentiator versus feature-parity AI checkboxes.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design

## Review Request

Requesting Market Research validation for differentiation and scope filter before engineering intake.

- Ticket: [BIN-72](/BIN/issues/BIN-72)
- Goal linkage: trusted AI adoption without compromising manuscript privacy
- Ask: approve/reframe with competitor-backed positioning

Assign to: Market Research
